speaker: MATT
raw_text: If I recall, you guys had taken a long rest after your previous battle, so now it's mid-morning, I'd say.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I forget, are we returning back to report in between missions, or are we doing a twofer?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I can send a message to the Gentleman, so we don't have to go back.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How can you do that? He's terribly far away.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I keep wanting to use this spell that I have, but I haven't been able to do it yet.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What spell's that?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's called Sending.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Sending?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I can send a message.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That is a literal description.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I guess that would cover it, then.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: But it's one of my most powerful spells, you guys, so if we're going to fight somebody, I don't know if it's smart.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Do you send an actual message, or is it a piece of paper?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's like a piece of paper of the mind.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Ooh.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You lost me a little.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you want me to do it?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Shouldn't we get out of the swamp first? What if there are scaries around?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: What time is it about, in the sky parts?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah. You don't have the ability to suddenly know where everything is that you've been. You have a good sense of it, and you can guide better than most, given most circumstances. But you also have not grown up in a swamp, and after a while, the pattern recognition begins to fade a bit and you discover, after you've been circling for a bit, stumbling upon your own footfalls, you've lost about two hours of travel in the middle of the swamp.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I throw little bits of moldy pastries at it because I feel sorry for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aww, feeding the plants.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And it throws it back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't seem to have much interest in them, unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's still really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It'll get hungry. Maybe later.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pressing on, you're heading back to Berleben, or are you searching for this hollow you found before?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was leading everyone back to where this troll was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make another survival check for me, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know exactly the direction of north, and I remember where we were, that is a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You know we're going to get lost in this swamp forever.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But I have perfect memory.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have firm memory of directions. You know which direction you're traveling, and you have--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But it all looks the same.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Yeah. You don't have the ability to suddenly know where everything is that you've been. You have a good sense of it, and you can guide better than most, given most circumstances. But you also have not grown up in a swamp, and after a while, the pattern recognition begins to fade a bit and you discover, after you've been circling for a bit, stumbling upon your own footfalls, you've lost about two hours of travel in the middle of the swamp.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I throw little bits of moldy pastries at it because I feel sorry for it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Aww, feeding the plants.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: And it throws it back.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Doesn't seem to have much interest in them, unfortunately.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Well, it's still really good.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: It'll get hungry. Maybe later.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Pressing on, you're heading back to Berleben, or are you searching for this hollow you found before?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I was leading everyone back to where this troll was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Make another survival check for me, please.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I know exactly the direction of north, and I remember where we were, that is a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: You know we're going to get lost in this swamp forever.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But I have perfect memory.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You have firm memory of directions. You know which direction you're traveling, and you have--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But it all looks the same.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: From the battles you've had and from watching the previous strike, you now notice the impact, and as the time progresses over the next round, the wounds are not knitting themselves back together. So, that finishes your turn. Are you going to move?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: There you go, come on.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: 12. Moving on up!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Ten to five?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Seven.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: What you got, Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fjord got a four.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: A fjour.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: A fjour by Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I think this is a surprise round. The troll cannot act this turn. We'll just let it go that way, because he'd be going up with you, Caleb, on the same turn. He's on the ground, dazed and stunned. What are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A Firebolt goes from my hand. That is a natural 20.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, come on now!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: With my Vax die, we will use this one. That is a three-- 11. Doubled is 22.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 22 points of fire damage. Nice. The bolt impacts, the flame curls across his now currently black, blasted chest and back area. The flames, as you noticed in a previous battle, has prevented its regeneration.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I didn't actually even want to come here. Ah!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: He can regenerate?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He can't; he's burned.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: They do normally, though.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fire!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: That is exactly what you needed. 18 points of poison damage to you, Yasha, and you are poisoned for one minute.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We had to have 100 gold, didn't we?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Does my Patient Defense do anything here?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is not about the gold, Fjord!

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: This is a reckoning!

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Just a straight roll, yeah? 11.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay, you just make it. The DC was 16.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hah. You're like, I made it.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I made it. You're telling me there's a chance.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. Fjord is unconscious. Brings us back, Caleb, it is now your turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, it comes back around to the next turn.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ah. Six seconds can go quick.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: As soon as it comes back, it heals ten hit points back up. So it came to consciousness. Just enough movement to get up from prone, knock Fjord down. It's going to make another swipe at Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Can it do that if it's Slowed?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No, it can't!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it still Slowed?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, thank god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's still Slowed, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, my god.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Oh man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You just saved Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That was two death saving throws right there.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, it was.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: And it had a third attack.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It had another one?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But it doesn't anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But it doesn't anymore.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yes! (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: It's like walking on the edge of a cliff in the middle of the night. And you didn't know how far the drop was, and you're just toeing the edge. No idea what was on the other side.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Unfortunately, the concussive blast of the dynamite shattered most of the bones and any materials that were in here, so what would've at one time been fairly easy to scavenge what remains, you gather from the patches of now dried and destroyed swamp ground that much of it has been scattered all across. You do manage to find some coins between the two of you-- mostly you, Nott-- and it takes some digging. You're basically having to pan for gold here. You're going in the muck and having to sift through your fingers and pour your water out a bit, take it into the water. You manage to find roughly 26 gold pieces, ten silver pieces, and let's say about 50 copper pieces.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Ah! Does anybody have anything glass or metal?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Put it in there!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: No, I'm not going to do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Why? It squirted poison!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's that big. It's about basketball size.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We can keep it!

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: What are you going to do with that? You don't want that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to sit on the ground. I'm going to look through my stuff, see if I have anything that can hold it.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to go through the skulls. Do you want to join me going through the skulls right now?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I was going to go to Caleb's aid, but seeing Beau walk away with him, I nod and go back to looking through the skulls for gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Let's not crowd the boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Both of you guys make investigation checks as you're looking through the rest of that. Yasha, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to start walking over towards where the skulls are.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: 20 for me.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Ten.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: No. That flask shattered on impact, sorry. You guys take a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: There's no sense repeating.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: So, Fjord, what are you doing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'm sitting there, watching Caleb and Yasha walk off and feeling sorry for myself.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. Caleb, you manage to snap out of it, and come back to reality.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I hand him one skin and I say  Here, this one's water. This one's booze. Take the water and I pour it out on one of my sashes and dab off his forehead a little bit. You all right?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Cool. Cool, cool, cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Y'all mind if we take a short rest or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's do that. There's a campfire over there that Yasha and I made with the oil and the torch that we threw. It's already done. Let's go sit by that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sit by the skulls!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a pile of bone and skull shards that is still burning and will be burning for a while from the oils.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's cozy over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I get my oil flask back too?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys finish your short rest. You're feeling a little more collected. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ja.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Cool. Cool, cool, cool.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Y'all mind if we take a short rest or something?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, let's do that. There's a campfire over there that Yasha and I made with the oil and the torch that we threw. It's already done. Let's go sit by that.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Sit by the skulls!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a pile of bone and skull shards that is still burning and will be burning for a while from the oils.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: It's cozy over here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Can I get my oil flask back too?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: No. That flask shattered on impact, sorry. You guys take a short rest.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll give out all the gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Whoa, you pass out the 26 gold?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, so we all get three. We all get three gold pieces. I'm keeping the extras.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: The downside of throwing dynamite into the reward pile.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I heal 39 points. I only have one hit dice left.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: All right. You guys continue making your way back towards Berleben. You made most of the journey and by the time the sky begins to subtly grow dark with the threat of dusk imminent in the next hour or so, you guys begin to make visual on the lantern lights in the outskirts of the northern side of the city. Within a short time. before it gets too dark in the swamp, you manage to make your way inside Berleben proper.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: All right, I guess we'll keep going.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, we should probably do that.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh wait, did they ever come back from their little walk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I imagine we wandered back.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, you shook it off.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Shook it off.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's great.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: How are you, Caleb?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Good. Good.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: You faded away there for a second. Are you okay?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, what happened? You good? Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: He gets weird when he kills people when he burns them. He did it before.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I think anyone would, really.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I don't know if Kiri would. She'd probably be cool with it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But Fjord is still here, so there is the important thing. Let's focus on the good things in our lives.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, thank you, Caleb. Appreciate that.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: You took care of the problem.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I feel like those two aren't mutually exclusive or anything, but yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Let's start walking!

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: All right.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's just getting awkward.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "I've been here for over a week, so no. But I'm sure there was. How long should I stay? What is the current atmosphere there?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god, Horris! Oh, he's not dead!

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: He goes, "Well, word of a motley group of strangers spreads in whispers, and I knew it had to be you based on the description. My friends, you've come this way. This place is miserable. Oh, do not think that I don't appreciate what you've done for me, please."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, you're right on the nose. It's pretty miserable.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, this place sucks.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "As rough as this journey was, I'd rather be here than in prison. What word of Zadash has come? Is Dolan all right?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's fine. He was sitting pretty next to the other diplomats of the town.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, he seemed like he was doing pretty good when we saw him.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Except, of course, that they're making ready for war.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I've heard whispers of that as well."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Did you see that crazy army ride by? There was a lot of horses. A lot.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: A round of liquor for all of you guys will run you about three silver pieces. It's not very expensive.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: He's very dapper. He touches the hearts of all of us. Where would we be without Fjord?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: All gone. All dead.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I mean he's not that cool or anything! I mean you probably wouldn't be interested in him at all.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Course not, I'm sorry."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Are you interested in him?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Here're your keys." Strangely surrounded by a group of strangers, now accosting her with various degrees of insinuation. "Drinks-wise, we prefer more hard liquors, or more of an ale beer?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Hard.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Any surprise you have.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, only a couple of surprises at my disposal, but--"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Do you have any ginger ale, or fresh milk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She pulls up this dark green bottle, and uncaps it. "It's beer, but its practically ginger ale. Not very alcoholic." She hands it over to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: You can't drink in real life and you still won't drink on graph paper.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Well, the order goes out there, and pay upon delivery. It took me the better part of a month to get here."

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I should've said like the swamp, right? Your eyes are like stagnant water, green and-- no? Green and yellow in places.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I walk directly up to the bar.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They look like a troll took a shit in them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Can I help you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, I'm good on drinks. I was just curious what it is that you're reading.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Roll a persuasion check.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: For a question? Okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: She doesn't like us very much.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, she hates us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She's had a bad series of minutes with you guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Just a nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I'm reading a fine story."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Is it fiction?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "It is."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I am so starved for a good book. We've been in the wild for a while. I don't suppose there's-- I doubt it, but is there any place to find literature here?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "I had to order this from Zadash, unfortunately."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That sounds terrible. How long does it take for it to get here?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You guys manage to get your evening's rest. You gather your things, and the next morning--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm finishing this and I'll be right up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. You guys finish up your respective drinks.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I go last.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I feel like we only need three rooms.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: It's just an extra couple gold.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I leave two gold on the counter before I leave. For you no matter what, but if you tell me what it is, I promise I won't tell the rest of them. I just want to lord it over them.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She palms the two gold. She goes, "It's called Feathered Leather."

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Enjoy your evening. Cheers. And I head upstairs.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She pockets the gold happily.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's about Vax. (laughter)

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Some bard somewhere in the past has penned a story about a dark knight in leather and feathers.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've seen that Tumblr post.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: For the record, I tell none of you.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Essentially, over about five minutes of him showing you around, they're mostly general herbal remedies for day to day ailing elements. It looks like all the special stuff that he had at his disposal went into those potions. Everything else has been general helping, like melatonin. Things that help you a little bit.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: So if we were to take that 50 and possibly spend it on something in this shop, is there anything that we should take a look at?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Depends on what you're looking for. I have various balms and salves to help with headaches, wounds that are festering. Some that help with helping your manhood come to light, some that help you with sleeping if you're having insomnia--"

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's the manhood thing?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh, boy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: They help you-- sometimes guys have problems with the (tongue click) and then there's potions that can help with that.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Ah.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: My mom said.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm curious about the sleeping thing you have, and again, anything you're particularly proud of.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to look around the store.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I'm always excited for a surprise.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. It takes you upwards of ten minutes, not very long, until you find one that's roughly that size. It's not perfectly spherical. It's got its imperfections, but it's pretty smooth.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No. Something with a little bit of heft to it.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Do you need it for a spell?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Not exactly. I don't really have anything specific. I guess I'll know it when I see it. Something about yea big. You know, my father used to tell me that if a stone had a ring around it all the way, it was lucky.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: If a stone had a ring around it?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Yeah, like a natural formation, a coloration, right? It doesn't matter, though. I start looking for a stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Kiri, help us look for a stone.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A good one.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'll look with him.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: (chirping) Make an investigation check, one of you with advantage, if you're aiding the other.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I will make the check with your help, if that's okay.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Sure.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. That is a 13.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: With advantage.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh. That is a 14.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. What particularly are you looking for?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: A stone about as large as a malformed baseball, I suppose.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's about a week's worth of travel to get to Hupperdook from where you are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I've had some shady friends.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Hupperdook it is.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's "dook" it up.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I think they have one of the highest imports and exports for illicit substances. I don't know. That's just what I've heard.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay, let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Let's get it started.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys are making your way along the Rillway Road eastward from Berleben, heading from here along Rillway. Heading past north of Felderwin, hitting the Felderwin Tillage, and then there's a crossroads that leads to the northern Gravelway Path.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going that far--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're following the roads. If you guys are cutting away from the roads and going through the rough open fields, that can be done.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have a carriage, let's stay on the roads.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Via road, it's about 110 miles to the crosswo-- crossroads, and then 120 miles along the Gravelway Path.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To hewe? To the cwosswoads?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You may have been there, actually. The crossroads, from what your experience is, folks who are making long travels stop there and might set up shop for a day or two to sell or trade whatever wares they have before they continue on the rest of their journey. And people from Felderwin and the Felderwin Tillage head up there sometimes to set up shop if there are people that are traveling through.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: We're going that far--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: If you're following the roads. If you guys are cutting away from the roads and going through the rough open fields, that can be done.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We have a carriage, let's stay on the roads.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Via road, it's about 110 miles to the crosswo-- crossroads, and then 120 miles along the Gravelway Path.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: To hewe? To the cwosswoads?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's about a week's worth of travel to get to Hupperdook from where you are.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What's in the cwosswoads?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You'll find out when you get there. You haven't traveled there before.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Is it a village? We don't know.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I've been there, haven't I?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The next morning, you guys get yourselves back up into the cart and continue on further. You pass beyond the river and start making your way towards the crossroads before your second day comes to completion. Who's going to keep watch the next evening? All right, make the roll, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I'll do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I will also do it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both individually?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I will do it individually.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Both of you guys roll.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I like how this is like Final Fantasy traveling.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh, I got a five.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: That's better. For the evening's rest-- taking your breaks, keeping watch throughout the night-- no incident. At one moment, there is a shifting of movement in the distance. Fjord, you go and check, and there is a family of foxes that are making their way through the field southward away as it gets colder. They're starting to move southward, and you spook them off to get away from the traps.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Scat!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The next morning, you guys get yourselves back up into the cart and continue on further. You pass beyond the river and start making your way towards the crossroads before your second day comes to completion. Who's going to keep watch the next evening? All right, make the roll, guys.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Scat!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: But since you've left, just estimating right now, I'd say it's been about a little over a week as of now.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: There's been a string of kidnappings there in town.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, this is where you're going, and Nogvurot is this way.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay, so we can sort of avoid--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can go straight, or you can arc-- it'll take more time.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: But they're next-doorsies.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should avoid Nogvurot right now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can hit it on the way back. It's up to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. On the way back. On the way back.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: After we've already taken care of the stuff so we get paid. Do we know how much time we have left before our time is up with the Gentleman's timeline?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Uh, with the days of travel... I'd have to go do the math again, and I'm sure that the Internet knows and has been keeping track--

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think we're probably fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll check in with CritRoleStats to make sure--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And Caleb will tell the group.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: You have a month.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh, okay, so we can sort of avoid--

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can go straight, or you can arc-- it'll take more time.

preceding_turns.text: You can hit it on the way back. It's up to you.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: After we've already taken care of the stuff so we get paid. Do we know how much time we have left before our time is up with the Gentleman's timeline?

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: I think we're probably fine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll check in with CritRoleStats to make sure--

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: And Caleb will tell the group.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But since you've left, just estimating right now, I'd say it's been about a little over a week as of now.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: And we have a month.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: So. You wake up the next morning. You get the sense that, being the vigilant beast that you are, if anybody wanted to come close to inspect, immediately saw Yasha and went, "Nope!" You continue on the next day of travel. Heading upward. Northeast, further and further. You can begin to see the edge of the Silberquel Ridge, the mountain range that, when you were approaching the Labenda Swamp, began to rise and kind of loomed over the swamp, and then began to slowly get more distant. As you traveled southward the crossroads begins to come further into view again. The mountain range moving closer, more massive, kind of peeking over, this large-- it's very, very steep edges to the base of this mountain range. You get the sense that passage over or through is very specific. Or at least nowhere in visible sight. As you guys begin to crest into dusk for this next, fourth evening of travel, who's keeping watch?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Except for your eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: They're like the ocean.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Her eyes are like the sea.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Your eyes are like the swamp. Super muddy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Your eyes are like blue crayons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: One's like the swamp, one's like the sky.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah...

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs)

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. I mean, one's like more purpley, but I guess that's sky, or swamp?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's very like, very diverse biodomes in your eyes.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Ooh.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (whispers) How?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah...

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not biome. Biodome.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (laughs) Biodomes, you know?

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Like a... I think that's a film?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: You know, like-- Yeah. Waterworld.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Pauly Shore movie.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Let's go.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: We're off the rails. We're off the rails.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: All right. The evening passes without issue, actually. Keeping a watch--

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: You call that "without issue?"

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's natural. It's in my blood.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Touche.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: You're so cool, how do you fucking eat gravel so hard? Oh, man.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's in my blood, man. It's all a ruse.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: As all of you come to consciousness, you glance over and you can see the dark shape, once Caleb points out the direction where it was. And as you're coming around the side, Nott... because you did not roll a very good stealth--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. 15 now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: 15. You see a brief-- a split-second of movement. And you look and you see a crossbow bolt hit the edge of the cart--

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Near where Chent was?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Near where he was standing. Who is still dancing.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Oh, shit. No direction? Can I tell from its impact from whence it came?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Not from this distance, no.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Okay. (panicked breathing) Crossbow attack! Crossbow attack! Wake up everyone! I'll take them out single-handed!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: No, no, no, switch to the Cabbage Patch! And I start punching Fjord and Beau in the shoulders.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (groans)

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Shh! Quiet! Be quiet!

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to keep flanking around in the shadows, trying to stay out of trouble. Crossbow at the ready.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: I start quietly handing over everybody's mouth, "Wake up, there's an ambush!" I'm scared of Yasha but I do it anyway. "Wake up, there's an ambush!"

speaker: MATT
raw_text: The next two days go on without issue. Passing a few travelers heading southward, crossing your way, until eventually, the Silberquel Ridge grows more and more prominent in your line of sight. Following the path, the fields that surround the north road begin to give way to tall grass and trade for scrub land and rocks, the once-vanishing shape of the Silberquel Ridge mountains growing bigger and bigger, the numerous columns of mingling black-and-white smoke you can now see spill from the eastern side of the mountain, diffusing as they rise. As you travel even closer, you can make out the two-tiered layout of a city built into the mountain. One large region encircles the very base of the mountain, ahundred or more dark workshops and towers, billowing smoke from chimneys and stacks visible. A number of large constructions dot the open spaces between these towers, some oversized cannons or others like mobile siege towers. One resembles a wheeled, multi-tiered cart with numerous, wicked-looking bolt-throwers attached. As you begin to head in this direction, you can also see that this lower area, this industrial field of various engines of warfare that are in the process of being constructed, some in their skeletal phase of being built. To the base of the mountain above that, you can see two winding switchback roads as well as one very steep staircase that ascends to a second level, about a hundred or more feet above the first. Here you see a massive outcropping of leveled rock that holds the other half of the city. There along the ridge of the mountain shelf lies hundreds of buildings and arches, occasionally dappled with an autumn-colored tree. Behind this city mass, you see a shimmering waterfall that cascades from out of the center of the mountain, about 1000 feet up, that glimmers like silver-white light and the little bit of sun that's coming through the clouds. You've reached the outskirts of Hupperdook.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: This is so dumb. What color is it?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a basic canvas. It's a tan.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It is Oregon Trail.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: It's all about the dysentery.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's a tan, light brown.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: It's a Florida convertible.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: The Volvo of wagons.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Essentially. Ironic that you went looking to build that and it was already in the possible path. All right. So you guys pull that off.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Take the blankets.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Take the blankets. Make your way back to your cart. Finish up your evening's rest, and at morning light, continue your way northward along the road. All righty.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: (clopping sounds)

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: That's some tiny-ass hooves.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: For the sake of brevity, since we're getting towards the end of the evening--

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We should take the grain for our horses.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Horses eat grass.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Let's count the grains.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god! Let's travel north, for fuck's sake!

speaker: MATT
raw_text: It's mid-afternoon.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: But not podunk?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: It's not podunk, by any means.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: It's builded?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. It's well-built. It seems comfortable. The base area around the mountain as you begin to approach-- and you can see now where the clouds and bits of steam are mingling and rising up, mixing into the sky above you-- the colors here are fairly drab: browns, grays, dark graphite metals. And immediately, you can see the ground itself.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: (coughs) That's good water.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You can see the ground itself as you step in the area has a faint layer of soot where some of the burned materials that are pressed up into the air have come down to settle onto the ground surrounding it. What do you wish to do?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What time of day is it?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: Okay. You haven't learned that much about the history of Hupperdook. You know it's been here for a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (sing-songy) Don't fuck me, Gil.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He did it.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Fucked me pretty hard. That's three. Can I use my mystical erudition to get an advantage on history checks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll allow it this time, but usually, you have to decide that before you make the roll. It's not a lucky feat, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Deciding it now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But going forward--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not much better.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: At all. Nine.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: This city. It's been here for quite some time, so while you're not sure of how long that field has been there, you get the sense that this isn't a recent testing ground. It probably has been their general testing ground for any technology they're developing.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: (sing-songy) Don't fuck me, Gil.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: Yes!

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: He did it.

preceding_turns.text: Fucked me pretty hard. That's three. Can I use my mystical erudition to get an advantage on history checks?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: I'll allow it this time, but usually, you have to decide that before you make the roll. It's not a lucky feat, so.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Deciding it now.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: But going forward--

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Not much better.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: At all. Nine.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. You haven't learned that much about the history of Hupperdook. You know it's been here for a while.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: This scarred pot-marked line?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "We got about two hours."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Are you?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: No, we're not.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "See, that's going to get you far here. Honesty."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Self-deprecation? Oh, honesty.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Well, probably both, depending on the time of day. So, who are you, if I might ask?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We're the Mighty Nein.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: Travelers passing through to somewhere else.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "So you're not from around here? I like that. What brings you to Hupperdook?"

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We thought the name was funny.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: That is accurate.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Her brow furrows and a smile curls even wider across her face. "You're not wrong. You're not wrong. Well, I happen to be a local looking for a way to kick off some boredom. Mind me showing you around?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: I don't know. What's your name?

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "My name? Rissa. Pleasure to meet you, Mr.--?"

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Fjord.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Fjord. Mayhaps we be getting in some trouble, too."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I hear you people like to get in trouble here at Hupperdook.

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: Especially at night.

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Oh, no charge. Or buy me a round, or five, and we'll call it even."

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Well, at this time, it'll probably be about dusk.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's like 8 00. Crazy.

preceding_turns.speaker: TALIESIN
preceding_turns.text: We need to find a place to store our stuff and hopefully find an inn for the night, first.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "There's a few inns to pick from. I can take you around. Here, I'll lead you up the walkway. Follow me."

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's nice. Why? Let's kill her.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Do we have this tourism look to us?

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: For sure we do.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: We might as well be wearing fanny packs. All of us.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: We should get some fanny packs.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Like an amazing fanny haversack?

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: Oh my god. You don't know what's in there. Keep pulling stuff out.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: You guys begin to make your way up the switchback path, up the mountainside towards the upper edge of the shelf. Along that way, your new friend, Rissa, jumps up into the cart with you. She says, "I get the sense you don't know much about us here."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: How much?

speaker: MATT
raw_text: "Anyway, things are a bit busy around here. They're busy most of the time. General workday here is about ten to 12 hours. No weekends, no breaks like the rest of you lazy and lackadaisical folk."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What do you want?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: I'm going to keep an eye on her, because I think she's going to try to steal some of our stuff.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: Okay. "That's all I ask. Maybe help me fuck with a friend or two."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: What?

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: Just say yes.

preceding_turns.speaker: SAM
preceding_turns.text: She's just like us.

preceding_turns.speaker: TRAVIS
preceding_turns.text: We're visitors here.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: She just asked to fuck with a friend. Yes?

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Beau, you make really bad first impressions, too. She's basically you.

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: That's true.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: She looks to Beau and is like, "Yeah."

preceding_turns.speaker: MARISHA
preceding_turns.text: I nod back. Hmm, yeah.

preceding_turns.speaker: MATT
preceding_turns.text: "Anyway, don't worry. I know the place is a bit droll, but once the sun's down, it gets interesting."

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: During all of that yammering on the way up, I look over at Yasha and sigh out.

preceding_turns.speaker: ASHLEY
preceding_turns.text: Yeah. A little crazy?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: That's it.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Are you saying you are crazy? I mean, you did that in front of everybody. Are we having an agreement?

preceding_turns.speaker: LIAM
preceding_turns.text: (whispering) I am crazy.

preceding_turns.speaker: LAURA
preceding_turns.text: Oh. I can see that about you.
